content:
{
  "safety_audit": {
    "audited_at": "2026-05-30T09:50:00.000Z",
    "auditor": "safety-auditor",
    "plan_id": "pg-002-migration-runner",
    "plan_revision": 1,
    "status": "blocked",
    "scores": {
      "data_races": "4/5",
      "strict_concurrency": "3/5",
      "thread_safety": "5/5",
      "memory_leaks": "2/5",
      "information_leaks": "4/5"
    },
    "blockers": [
      {
        "id": "BLOCKER-1",
        "category": "memory_leaks",
        "severity": "high",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (proposed)",
        "line": "N/A — design artifact",
        "finding": "CLI entry point: PG pool never closed on error or success",
        "detail": "The PG path of runMigrations() calls initPg(url, ssl) which creates a pg.Pool, then calls applyMigrations(client). Neither on success nor on error (the .catch handler in the CLI block) is pool.end() called. For PGlite mode, the in-memory instance is garbage collected, but for node-postgres mode (real PG), the Pool holds TCP connections and event loop handles. A failed migration will leak the pool until process exit. For sequential CLI usage this is minor, but if runMigrations is called programmatically in a long-lived process, pools will accumulate.",
        "reproduction": "1. Set OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@host/db\n2. Run migrate.ts with a broken migration SQL file\n3. Migration fails, error logged, process.exit(1)\n4. Pool connections are never closed — connection leak on each invocation",
        "fix": "Add a finally block or explicit close: wrap the PG client acquisition in a Scope or use a try/finally to call await client.end() or pool.end(). For the CLI entry point, process.exit closes handles, but for the programmatic API this is a definite leak."
      },
      {
        "id": "BLOCKER-2",
        "category": "data_races",
        "severity": "high",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts",
        "line": "31-37",
        "finding": "PGlite migration path lacks transaction wrapping — partial migration corrupts database",
        "detail": "applyMigrations() in db.pg.ts has two paths: (a) PGlite path: reads migrations via readMigrationFiles() and manually executes each SQL statement via client.exec() in a for loop with no transaction. If statement N of 3 fails, statements 1..N-1 are already committed. This leaves the schema in an indeterminate state. (b) node-postgres path: uses drizzle-orm's migrate() which wraps in a transaction — safe. The plan reuses this same applyMigrations() for the PG path in migrate.ts. Drizzle's readMigrationFiles() returns migrations sorted by folder name (timestamp prefix), so ordering IS correct. But the lack of transaction wrapping for PGlite is a data integrity risk.",
        "reproduction": "1. runMigrations({ dialect: 'pg' }) with PGlite (in-memory or file-based)\n2. Inject a migration with a broken SQL statement in the middle\n3. The first SQL statements execute successfully, then statement N fails\n4. Schema is partially applied — no rollback",
        "fix": "Wrap the PGlite migration loop in a PGlite transaction: await client.exec('BEGIN'); try { ... loop ... await client.exec('COMMIT'); } catch { await client.exec('ROLLBACK'); throw; }"
      },
      {
        "id": "BLOCKER-3",
        "category": "data_races",
        "severity": "high",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.pg.sql.ts",
        "line": "1",
        "finding": "PG schema files import from drizzle-orm/sqlite-core — DB:generate:pg would produce incorrect PG DDL",
        "detail": "The .pg.sql.ts bridge files re-export from .sql.ts files that use drizzle-orm/sqlite-core (sqliteTable, text, integer from sqlite-core). schema.pg.sql.ts explicitly imports integer from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core'. drizzle.config.pg.ts with dialect: 'postgresql' pointing at schema: './src/**/*.pg.sql.ts' will traverse these re-exports back to sqlite-core definitions. While Drizzle Kit may translate some types (text → text), it cannot generate proper PG types like jsonb, boolean[], or native PG serial columns from sqlite-core schema definitions. The existing migration-pg/ DDL (20260529023435_yellow_molly_hayes/migration.sql) uses jsonb, boolean, and proper PG foreign keys — these were hand-crafted or one-off generated, NOT produced by drizzle-kit from sqlite-core. Running db:generate:pg would produce incompatible or wrong DDL.",
        "reproduction": "1. bun run db:generate:pg (drizzle.config.pg.ts)\n2. Drizzle Kit traverses sqliteTable() re-exports and generates PG DDL\n3. Generated DDL uses PG text/integer equivalents, not proper PG types (no jsonb, boolean fallback to integer)\n4. DDL is incompatible with the existing migration-pg/ DDL that already used native PG types",
        "fix": "Rewrite .pg.sql.ts files to use pgTable() from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core' with PG-native column types. Do NOT re-export from .sql.ts files — define independent PG schemas that mirror the SQLite schemas but with PG type annotations."
      }
    ],
    "warnings": [
      {
        "id": "WARN-1",
        "category": "memory_leaks",
        "severity": "medium",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (proposed)",
        "finding": "PG path in runMigrations() never closes client/Pool after success",
        "detail": "For the PG success path: initPg() creates a Pool (or PGlite instance), applyMigrations() runs, then the function returns without calling pool.end() or closing the PGlite client. In CLI mode this is masked by process.exit(0), but the programmatic API (runMigrations()) will leak connections. PGlite instances also hold WASM memory and file handles that should be released.",
        "fix": "Return a close/dispose function or use Effect's Scope to automatically close the PG client after migration completes."
      },
      {
        "id": "WARN-2",
        "category": "data_races",
        "severity": "medium",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts",
        "line": "32-38",
        "finding": "getPath() depends on Effect runtime (Global.Path) — may fail if called before Effect init",
        "detail": "The plan acknowledges this risk (in the Impact Analysis table): 'db.ts has Effect runtime dependencies. Calling getPath() before Effect is initialized could fail.' The plan's fallback to process.env.OPENCODE_SQLITE_DB_PATH or 'Global.Path.data + \"/opencode.db\"' is mentioned but not actually implemented in the design code. The provided migrate.ts code calls getPath() unconditionally for the SQLite path without a try/catch.",
        "fix": "In getPath(), wrap Global.Path access in try/catch with fallback to 'opencode.db' in cwd, or make getPath Effect-aware."
      },
      {
        "id": "WARN-3",
        "category": "information_leaks",
        "severity": "medium",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (proposed)",
        "finding": "process.env.OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL read at module/lambda call time bypasses credential isolation",
        "detail": "The security doctrine (database_security_doctrine.v1.json) states: 'OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL must NOT be set as a process-level env var. It must be read via Effect Config (Config.redacted).' The plan's detectDialect() function and runMigrations() PG path both read process.env.OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL directly. This bypasses the security model that establishes credential isolation via Effect's Redacted type. Additionally, when OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is set as a process-level env var, it leaks to subprocesses through shell.ts and lsp/server.ts (documented in security doctrine).",
        "fix": "Read DIALECT and DATABASE_URL through Effect Config, not process.env. For the CLI migration tool, accept connection string as a CLI flag (--connection-string) that's explicitly scoped and never leaks to subprocesses."
      },
      {
        "id": "WARN-4",
        "category": "strict_concurrency",
        "severity": "low",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (proposed)",
        "finding": "CLI --db-path flag parsed but not handled for PG dialect use",
        "detail": "The CLI parser extracts --db-path from args but the runMigrations() signature doesn't pass it to the PG path — it's only used for SQLite via dbPath. The PG path reads opts.connectionString or falls back to process.env.OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL. A user passing --pg --db-path /custom/path would silently fall back to the env var, which is surprising. Similarly, there's no --connection-string flag for PG override.",
        "fix": "Add --connection-string flag for PG URL override, or document that --db-path only applies to SQLite dialect."
      },
      {
        "id": "WARN-5",
        "category": "information_leaks",
        "severity": "low",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (proposed)",
        "finding": "Error message in CLI may leak connection string on migration failure",
        "detail": "The CLI catch handler: console.error('Migration failed:', err). If the underlying error from applyMigrations() or initPg() includes the connection URL (e.g., Pool connection failure includes the host/port/database), the connection string (containing password) could appear in the console output. This is a defense-in-depth concern.",
        "fix": "Add a sanitizing error handler that strips connection string patterns from error messages before logging, or use a structured error type that doesn't serialize the connection URL."
      }
    ],
    "verified_safe": [
      {
        "item": "Migration ordering guarantees",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts",
        "detail": "readMigrationFiles() from drizzle-orm/migrator returns migrations sorted by their folder name (timestamp prefix like '20260529023435_yellow_molly_hayes'). Drizzle generates timestamp-prefixed folder names that sort chronologically. This ordering is correct and matches how drizzle-kit's built-in migrate() works."
      },
      {
        "item": "SQLite vs PGlite migration folder separation",
        "detail": "The SQLite path reads from ./migration/ folder, the PG path reads from ./migration-pg/ folder via new URL('../../migration-pg', import.meta.url).pathname in applyMigrations(). These are independent directories — no risk of applying SQLite DDL to PGlite or vice versa."
      },
      {
        "item": "#db import alias resolves to correct file",
        "file": "packages/opencode/package.json",
        "line": "29",
        "detail": "The #db import alias maps to ./src/storage/db.pg.ts for both bun and node. init() and applyMigrations() are both exported from db.pg.ts. The destructuring import { init as initPg, applyMigrations } from '#db' is correct."
      },
      {
        "item": "npm script names — no collisions",
        "file": "packages/opencode/package.json",
        "detail": "The new script names (migrate, migrate:pg, db:generate, db:generate:pg) do not overlap with any existing script names. The existing db script is preserved unchanged."
      },
      {
        "item": "CI Postgres service container database creation",
        "file": ".github/workflows/test.yml (proposed)",
        "detail": "POSTGRES_DB: opencode_test in the service container's env block causes Postgres to create the 'opencode_test' database on startup. The connection string postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/opencode_test will connect to it successfully."
      },
      {
        "item": "CI job isolation — no impact on existing jobs",
        "file": ".github/workflows/test.yml (proposed)",
        "detail": "The pg job is a new job that runs after e2e. It does not modify any shared CI state that existing jobs depend on. The unit and e2e jobs remain unchanged."
      },
      {
        "item": "PGlite vs node-postgres dual init in db.pg.ts",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts",
        "line": "12-23",
        "detail": "init() correctly auto-detects PGlite mode (:memory:, /path, file:path) vs real PG (URL with host). PGlite gets its own drizzle instance, real PG gets node-postgres drizzle. The duck-type check in applyMigrations() via (db as any).$client.exec matches this dual-init pattern correctly."
      },
      {
        "item": "No module-level side effects in proposed migrate.ts",
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (proposed)",
        "detail": "The proposed migrate.ts has no module-level side effects. All imports are static, and the only runtime work happens inside runMigrations() or the if (import.meta.main) guard. This is correct for a CLI entry point."
      }
    ],
    "out_of_scope_findings": [
      {
        "file": "packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.pg.sql.ts",
        "finding_type": "debt",
        "summary": "PG bridge files import from drizzle-orm/sqlite-core — whole .pg.sql.ts pattern needs refactoring"
      }
    ]
  }
}
